_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-3 Medication Shortage (Revised 3/31/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-3: Medication Shortage — MDHHS Approved 3/31/2025
purpose: Govern EMS operations and patient care during medication shortages. Defines shortage categories, provider and agency obligations, and MCA responsibilities. Medication shortages may affect all care levels. MCAs must establish processes for managing shortages and communicating alternatives to providers.

sections.title: Medication Shortage Definitions
points: Type 1 — National Shortage: FDA or manufacturer declares nationwide shortage; may affect all EMS systems nationally
Type 2 — State Shortage: MDHHS declares statewide shortage affecting Michigan EMS systems
Type 3 — Regional Shortage: shortage affecting a specific region or group of MCAs within Michigan
Type 4 — MCA Shortage: shortage declared at the MCA level; may affect all or a subset of agencies within the MCA
Type 5 — Agency Shortage: shortage affecting a single EMS agency; agency must notify MCA immediately
Type 6 — Vehicle/Unit Shortage: shortage limited to a specific vehicle or unit; agency must address within established timeframe
Type 7 — Temporary Shortage: intermittent or anticipatory shortage with an expected resolution timeframe

sections.title: EMS Provider and Agency Obligations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-3: Medication Shortage — Provider and Agency Obligations
points: EMS agencies must immediately notify the MCA when a shortage is identified at the agency or unit level
Providers must not knowingly deploy without required medications unless authorized by MCA shortage protocols
Providers must document any case in which a medication shortage affected patient care or treatment capability
Agencies must implement MCA-directed shortage protocols and communicate availability status to crews
Providers must follow online medical control guidance when a shortage impacts treatment options for a specific patient

sections.title: MCA Responsibilities During Shortage
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-3: Medication Shortage — MCA Responsibilities
points: MCAs must establish written shortage response plans as part of their system operations
When a shortage is identified, the MCA must notify affected agencies and providers as soon as practicable
MCA Medical Director must authorize any protocol modifications or alternative agents during a shortage
MCAs must communicate shortage status and authorized alternatives to MDHHS per MDHHS reporting requirements
MCAs must document shortage events including duration, scope, alternatives authorized, and patient care impacts

deterioration_flags: Required medication unavailable — notify online medical control immediately; document shortage impact in PCR
scope_notes: Seven shortage types defined: National, State, Regional, MCA, Agency, Vehicle/Unit, Temporary
Shortage impacting patient care must be documented in PCR
MCA Medical Director must authorize alternative agents
MCA must report shortage status to MDHHS
